what_it_does: This file is a metadata record for a channel attachment: it declares an attachment identifier, channel, attachment type, target board item URI, and creation timestamp.
observations: The file contains only YAML frontmatter and no body content
The target is a board:// URI pointing to item-826fc0f7
The added timestamp is ISO 8601 with timezone offset
standouts: Target uses a board:// URI scheme for a board item
Timestamp includes microsecond precision and UTC offset
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter for structured metadata
Attachment id uses an att- prefix
Target entity is referenced by a URI-style identifier
Creation time recorded as ISO 8601 timestamp
concerns: File contains no payload or content beyond metadata
No author or updater field is present
No indication whether the target board item is still valid
notes: A metadata stub that records a board-items attachment in the dragnet-development channel, pointing to board item item-826fc0f7.
questions: What content or artifact is actually attached?
Is the referenced board item still active or valid?
Are there other attachments to the same board item?
purpose_vs_actual: matches — the file's metadata records an attachment association, which is exactly what it appears designed to do
intention_signals: It seems intended to create a durable, queryable record that associates a channel attachment with a specific board item for later retrieval and traceability.
contribution: Provides a timestamped, typed link between a channel attachment and a board item, enabling traceability from channel memory to board content without claiming primacy over other records.
summary_for_embedding: A YAML-frontmatter channel attachment record in the dragnet-development channel of type board_items, linking attachment att-16b9a86c to board item item-826fc0f7 and recording its creation timestamp.
intention_for_embedding: To persist the association between a channel attachment and a target board item for retrieval and audit.
